Here you go: Ingredients: 2 cups all-purpose flour 1 teaspoon salt 1/2 cup water Instructions: 1. In a large bowl, whisk together the flour and salt. 2. Add the water and mix until the dough comes together and forms a ball. 3. Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface and knead for 5-7 minutes, until it is smooth and elastic. 4. Place the dough in a greased bowl and cover with plastic wrap. Let the dough rest for at least 30 minutes, or up to overnight. 5. After the dough has rested, divide it into 4 equal pieces. Roll out each piece of dough into a thin sheet, about 1/8 inch thick. 6. Cut the dough into your desired shape and size. 7. Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil. 8. Add the pasta and cook for 2-3 minutes, or until al dente. 9. Drain the pasta and serve immediately with your favorite sauce.

Drop the dough by rounded tablespoons onto the prepared baking sheet. 6. Bake for 10-12 minutes, or until the edges are golden brown and the centers are set. 7. Here it is: 1 cup chopped onions 1 cup chopped celery 1/2 cup chopped carrots 2 tablespoons chopped garlic 1/4 cup butter 3 tablespoons all-purpose flour 4 cups chicken broth 1 cup dry white wine 1/2 teaspoon salt 1/4 teaspoon dried thyme 1/4 teaspoon black pepper 1/4 teaspoon cayenne pepper 1 1/2 pounds lobster meat, cut into 1-inch pieces 1 cup heavy cream 1 tablespoon chopped fresh parsley Instructions: In a large pot or Dutch oven over medium heat, saute the onions, celery, carrots, and garlic in the butter until softened. Sprinkle the flour over the vegetables and cook, stirring constantly, for 2 minutes. Add the chicken broth, wine, salt, thyme, black pepper, and cayenne pepper and bring to a boil. Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 30 minutes. Add the lobster meat and cook for 5 minutes. Add the cream and parsley and cook until heated through. Serve immediately.

Preheat oven to 375 degrees F (190 degrees C). 2. In a large skillet, brown the ground beef and Italian sausage. Drain excess fat. 3.
